description: LLmap variant-priors schema. Layer 4 of the knowledge stack: population SV/SNV catalogs ingested once and consumed deterministically at mapping time.

database_catalog.description: Supported variant databases. Each source/<name>.json supplies an ingest spec adhering to this entry shape.

source_spec_schema.description: Schema for one sources/<id>.json file. See sources/ for live specs.

fields.name: expected_count
fields.note: approximate variant record count for sanity check

fields.name: ingest_command
fields.note: pseudocode bcftools/bedtools pipeline producing the prior table

per_position_prior_format.description: Per-position record after ingest. One physical row per genomic bucket (default 100 bp). See PRIOR_FORMAT.md.

aggregation_rules.description: How records from multiple catalogs combine into a single per-position prior.

snv.n_alt_alleles: max() across catalogs at same position
snv.max_af: max() across catalogs at same position
snv.rationale: dbSNP / gnomAD / ClinVar are overlapping populations; take the upper-bound AF as the most defensible prior.

sv.n_overlapping: sum() of unique SVs after dedup by (TYPE, start_bucket, end_bucket) at +/-1 bucket tolerance
sv.summary: union of TYPE:AF tuples, keeping max AF per TYPE per bucket

aggregation_rules.tie_breaking: When AF is missing in one catalog and present in another, prefer the catalog with explicit AF. Pure presence (e.g. DGV) contributes only to n_*; AF stays 0.0.

consumed_by.description: Runtime consumers of the prior table.

entries.uses: snv_max_af + sv_max_af to scale mismatch / soft-clip penalty
sv_summary to recognise that a candidate is a known polymorphism
full vector as auxiliary input channel during training
